_comment: Production manifest for Issue 1. Three concerns are separated: assets (what exists), panels (what is in each frame), render (how to generate and assemble). Gemini receives assets + scene intent. Canva receives image outputs + text layer specs. Text is never Gemini's job.
comic: AI Isn't My Cup of Tea
subtitle: LEO — the First Business Computer

palette.description: Warm cream background, sepia-brown ink lines, no cool greys. Extracted from approved page 1.

simmons.visual: Early 30s, wire-rimmed round spectacles, neat dark hair, jacket and tie. Expression: precise, analytical, patient.

clerk_woman.visual: Young woman, 1940s dress, green eyeshade, fingers on calculator keys. Expression: blank, automatic.

nippy.visual: Black-and-white uniform, white apron, small cap. Composed, professional, warm.

manager.visual: Male, dark suit, clipboard. Walks the aisle between desks.

office_boy.visual: Young, vest and shirt, sorting leather bags on a table. Focused.

cadby_hall_exterior.description: Grand red-brick Edwardian factory-fortress. Morning fog. Delivery lorry. Sign: J. LYONS & CO.

corner_house_interior.description: Chandeliers, white tablecloths, live band on balcony. Packed with ordinary working people. Warm light. Nippies moving between tables.

food_factory.description: Industrial cake production. Conveyor belts, metal trays, baking machines. One or two workers supervising. Clean and mechanical.

checking_dept.description: Rows of Burroughs adding machines receding to a vanishing point. Wooden partitions between desks. Green eyeshades. Stacks of bills. Locked leather bags. Through a far doorway: another room just as full.

boardroom.description: Lyons boardroom. Long table, senior men seated. A chart on the wall showing teashop numbers growing year-on-year.

simmons_office_night.description: A single desk, single lamp, everyone else gone. Window shows London at night. Papers and reports on the desk.

teashop_self_service_1945.description: Same elegant Lyons teashop room — but no Nippies. Self-service counter. A woman in ordinary clothes serves herself. The room feels emptied of something.

burroughs_calculator.description: 1930s Burroughs adding machine. Hand crank on the right side, rows of keys, paper roll printing at top.

farthing.description: A single Victorian/Edwardian farthing coin. Small, copper-brown.

leather_bags.description: Locked leather satchel bags, identical, each marked with a shop number. Piled on a long table.

notebook_1923.description: Open notebook, careful handwriting: 'The day will come when machines will do this work.' Underlined twice. Date in margin: 1923.

memo_1947.description: A typed memo. Partially visible text: '...advanced office machinery... United States... Thompson and Standingford...'

typography._comment: All text is added in Canva as type layers. Never passed to Gemini.
typography.caption_font: Courier New or similar slab serif
typography.caption_style: italic, small, cream box with brown border
typography.speech_bubble_style: white oval, black border, tail pointing to speaker
typography.narrator_box_style: brown border, cream background, robot icon left

pages.purpose: Lyons is magnificent. Democratic luxury. But a shadow: one farthing profit per cup.
pages.reader_takeaway: How does something this big run on a farthing?

story_so_far_box.speaker: robot_narrator
story_so_far_box.text: A long time ago, before computers could talk, humans had to do all the boring work themselves. My friends weren't around to help yet. This is where it starts.

this_issue_box.text: A room full of people doing machine work. A farthing's profit. And one man who sees what nobody else can see.

panels.label: Exterior Cadby Hall
panels.scene_intent: Arriving at Cadby Hall on a foggy 1935 morning. Workers in overcoats. A Lyons delivery lorry at the gates.

canva_text_layers.caption: Cadby Hall, Hammersmith. 1935. Britain's largest catering empire.

panels.label: Corner House dining room
panels.scene_intent: The warmth and grandeur of a Corner House full of ordinary people. Chandeliers and white tablecloths — democratic luxury.

canva_text_layers.caption: Two hundred teashops. A hundred and fifty million meals a year. High tea — for everyone.

panels.label: The farthing
panels.scene_intent: Nippy's white-gloved hand placing a cup of tea on a white tablecloth. Foreground: a single farthing coin.

canva_text_layers.caption: One farthing. The profit on every cup.

panels.emotional_note: The shadow falls. Reader asks: how does that even work?

pages.purpose: Two factories. One makes cakes with machines. One makes numbers with people.
pages.emotional_arc: The parallel lands → scale overwhelms → the farthing connects
pages.reader_takeaway: Behind every cup of tea, 300 people are doing work that machines should do.

panels.label: Food factory — machines
panels.scene_intent: Cadby Hall food factory. Conveyor belts, trays of cakes, industrial baking machines. Clean, efficient. This is how a modern factory looks.

canva_text_layers.caption: One factory made cakes. Machines did the work.

panels.label: Number factory — people
panels.scene_intent: The Checking Department at the same industrial scale as the food factory — but every machine is a person. Rows of women at calculators behind partitions. Same framing as p2_f1, deliberate visual mirror.

canva_text_layers.caption: One factory made numbers. People did the work.

panels.label: Scale — clerks hall wide
panels.scene_intent: The Checking Department to a vanishing point. Three hundred women. Through a far doorway: a second room just as large. The scale is crushing.

canva_text_layers.caption: Three hundred clerks. One job: check the waitresses' bills. All day. Every day.

panels.emotional_note: Overwhelming scale. And through that door — there is more.

panels.label: Leather bags
panels.scene_intent: Office boy sorting locked leather bags by shop number on a long table. Focused, methodical. The bags are identical and numbered.

canva_text_layers.caption: Two hundred and fifty shops. Every morning. Same bags. Same numbers.

panels.label: Hands and farthing
panels.scene_intent: Close on a woman's hands cranking a Burroughs calculator. On the desk beside it: a single farthing coin. A teashop bill being checked.

canva_text_layers.caption: A farthing. That is what this costs.

panels.emotional_note: Callback to page 1. Now the farthing has weight.

pages.purpose: One person sees this as cruelty and has known the answer for twelve years.
pages.emotional_arc: The structural trap → private conviction → human face → solitary patience → one word
pages.reader_takeaway: Simmons has been waiting 12 years. He knows. The world just hasn't caught up.

panels.label: Boardroom trap
panels.scene_intent: Simmons at a boardroom chart. Senior men seated. The chart shows shops growing — but so does the cost. Calm, tense atmosphere.

canva_text_layers.caption: More shops. More clerks. More cost. Smaller margin.

speech_bubbles.text: Only if we hire more clerks.

panels.emotional_note: The structural trap. Collapsing under its own weight.

panels.label: The notebook — 1923
panels.scene_intent: Close on the open notebook. The handwriting is careful, precise. The words and date are the only content of the panel.

canva_text_layers.caption: He had written those words twelve years ago.

panels.emotional_note: THE key beat. Twelve years of private certainty.

panels.label: The Nippy — human face
panels.scene_intent: A Nippy moving between tables with a tray, composed and precise. Warm room. She is unaware of the floors above her. This is the best of what Lyons offers.

canva_text_layers.caption: She had no idea what it cost to count everything she served.

panels.emotional_note: The human face of the problem. Robot narrator's empathy.

panels.label: Simmons alone, late
panels.scene_intent: Simmons at his desk, late evening, single lamp, reading a thick report. Not unhappy — patient. London visible through the window behind him.

canva_text_layers.caption: He had rationalised everything he could. It was never enough.

panels.emotional_note: Long patience. A man who has not given up.

panels.label: One word: Automatic
panels.scene_intent: Simmons's hand writing one word at the foot of a page: 'Automatic.' Underlined. Nothing else in frame.
panels.emotional_note: No caption. The word does everything. Page ends here.

pages.purpose: Time compresses. The war abolished the problem — but a door is opening.
pages.emotional_arc: Where it began → long effort → bitter irony → the door opens
pages.reader_takeaway: The answer is out there. Two men are about to go find it.

panels.label: 1923 — first week
panels.scene_intent: FLASHBACK. 1923. Young Simmons, fresh-faced, being shown to a desk. Beside him: a man at a Dickensian high writing desk entering figures by hand into a ledger. Simmons stares at the man with the ledger.

panels.label: 1937 — rationalisation
panels.scene_intent: 1937. Systems Research team around a table, flowcharts on a board. More organised. Still people. Still human arithmetic at the core.

canva_text_layers.caption: Fourteen years. The most efficient clerical operation in the world. Still not enough.

panels.label: 1945 — bitter irony
panels.scene_intent: 1945. The same elegant Lyons teashop — but no Nippies, no checking department. Self-service. A woman in plain clothes serves herself. The room feels emptied.

canva_text_layers.caption: The war took the Nippies. And the job Simmons had spent twenty years dreaming of automating — it no longer existed.

panels.emotional_note: Bitter irony. Problem abolished by accident, not solved.

panels.label: 1947 — the memo
panels.scene_intent: 1947. Simmons picks up a memo from his desk. His expression shifts — something long-patient beginning to stir. The memo text is partially visible.

canva_text_layers.caption: 1947. A memo. Two men. A ship. And somewhere in America — the answer.

panels.emotional_note: Forward momentum. The door is opening.

canva_text_layers.text: Next issue: Two men sail to America with a question nobody has thought to ask yet. And in a laboratory in New Jersey, the answer is already being built.

render_pipeline._comment: How to go from this spec to finished pages.

step_1_generate_panels.for_each_panel: Assemble Gemini call from: scene_intent + characters[].visual + environment.description + props[].description
step_1_generate_panels.reference_images: Pass: characters[].file (where not null) + palette.file + environment.reference_files
step_1_generate_panels.do_not_ask_gemini_for: captions, speech bubbles, header text, coming_next text — these are all Canva layers

step_2_canva_assembly.place_panels: Position generated images per panel.position in layout
step_2_canva_assembly.normalise_tone: Apply single Canva filter across all panels on the page
step_2_canva_assembly.add_text_layers: All canva_text_layers fields — captions, speech bubbles, header, coming_next
